items.title: Angus Taylor refuses to commit to a multicultural Australia under repeated questioning
items.summary: The opposition leader, pressed multiple times on whether his party still supported multiculturalism or endorsed One Nation leader Pauline Hanson's vision of a "monoculture", refused to commit to either.

items.title: Jail for man who sexually assaulted 76-year-old in Coffs aged care home
items.summary: A 76-year-old woman was left "crying buckets" after being sexually assaulted in a Mid North Coast aged care home, a court hears. Her attacker has now been jailed for 12 years.

items.title: Judge excludes video of former Sydney nurse allegedly threatening Israeli patients from case
items.summary: Lawyers argued the viral video clip from an online chat with an Israeli influencer breached NSW laws prohibiting recording private conversations without consent.

items.title: 'Shaman' and partner withheld magic mushrooms use before Adelaide fashion icon's death, inquest told
items.summary: A coronial inquest is investigating the death of Adelaide fashion icon Carol Foord, who used magic mushrooms as an alternative treatment prior to her death in June 2024.

items.title: Wagga Wagga man fined for selling horse meat to greyhound trainers
items.summary: A Wagga Wagga man will be more than $40,000 out of pocket after selling tonnes of horse meat to dog trainers in NSW and Victoria.

items.title: SA government moves to regulate AI data centres as water, energy concerns mount
items.summary: The South Australian government is pushing for the state to become home to billions of dollars' worth of artificial intelligence data centres, but says it will seek to regulate their water and energy use.

items.title: Tasmanian egg producers brace for deadly strain of bird flu, potential mass euthanasia
items.summary: Tasmanian poultry producers are ramping up biosecurity measures, as they prepare for the arrival of a deadly strain of bird flu. The state government has stressed that it's well prepared, but says euthanasia is "definitely possible" if there's an outbreak in commercial poultry.

items.title: Virgin Australia allows pets in plane cabin in expansion of Melbourne-Queensland pilot
items.summary: An Australian airline is allowing small cats and dogs on a range of flights, with other passengers appearing unconcerned about sharing the plane with furry creatures.

items.title: Sunshine Coast students push back as Australia positions itself as a global AI leader
items.summary: As Australia tracks toward becoming a global leader in artificial intelligence infrastructure, a group of high school students is demanding stronger regulation.

items.title: Disability advocates welcome extension after 'ridiculous and disrespectful' NDIS inquiry
items.summary: Disability advocates welcome an extension to a Senate inquiry probing a bill paving the way for the biggest-ever cuts to the NDIS.

items.title: Dead birds to be tested for H5N1 bird flu after washing up on remote SA coast
items.summary: Three dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, as well as another two wild birds in Western Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

items.title: ACEREZ investigation finds failures during construction led to destruction of Aboriginal rock shelter
items.summary: ACEREZ introduces strict cultural heritage protocols after a rock shelter was irreparably damaged.

items.title: New spider discovered in Queensland uses 'ballista' web to trap prey
items.summary: A bizarre species of spider from the rainforests of Far North Queensland springs a nasty surprise.

items.title: The winners and losers in the 2026 NSW budget
items.summary: With billions for health but limited funds for the state's regions, here are the 2026 NSW budget's winners and losers.

items.title: Indian medical exam leaks scandal sparks a resit, protests and tragedy
items.summary: More than 2 million students across India have resat the country's highly competitive medical entrance exam after last month's test was cancelled amid allegations that questions had been leaked.

items.title: Lila McGuire celebrates success of First Nations talent in Logie nominations
items.summary: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

items.title: The Doyles $20k award-winning artwork revealed as 'blatant copy'
items.summary: The Doyles award committee is investigating why it took nearly 12 months to discover a winning landscape painting was an imitation of another artist's work.

items.title: How one letter gifted Cheryl her Indigenous identity — and two siblings
items.summary: Cheryl van Wageningen was shocked to receive a letter revealing that not only was she Indigenous, but she had a brother and a sister.

items.title: Greens back CGT, negative gearing changes in return for extended NDIS inquiry, end to super loophole
items.summary: The Greens pledge their support in exchange for an end to an exemption that allows self-managed super funds to borrow money to invest in residential property, and a delay on the overhaul of the NDIS.

items.title: Health warning issued after powerful sedative etomidate was found in illegal vapes in Victoria
items.summary: Victorian health authorities say vape products containing the powerful sedative etomidate have been found being sold illegally.

items.title: Victoria's Doreen Egg farm admits to misleading free-range labelling
items.summary: Doreen Egg admits it sold eggs labelled as "free range" despite its hens not having access to an outdoor range, as required under Free Range Egg Labelling Information Standard.

items.title: KPMG chair Martin Sheppard resigns amid whistleblower fallout
items.summary: KPMG chair Martin Sheppard and two audit partners are leaving the accounting and consulting giant in the wake of a scandal over misuse of confidential client information and the alleged mistreatment of a whistleblower.

items.title: Thirteen killed and dozens injured in blast in Qatar's Ras Laffan gas processing facility
items.summary: The Sunday night blast in Qatar's Ras Laffan gas processing facility is one of the deadliest events recorded in gas industry.

items.title: Pauline Hanson says she supports paid parental leave after questioning pay for women 'not at work'
items.summary: Pauline Hanson has clarified she would not seek to cut government-funded paid parental leave if One Nation were in power after opening the door to a wind-back at her National Press Club address last week.

items.title: Former US Federal Reserve chair Alan Greenspan dies aged 100
items.summary: The wife of the former federal reserve chair says he died from complications of Parkinson's disease.

items.title: Farmers say absentee landholders hindering battle to control feral pests
items.summary: Farmers says some absentee landholders, who do not live on the land full-time, are not pulling their weight when it comes to feral pest management.

items.title: Children in hot car among at least 18 dead in France as Europe bakes
items.summary: At least 18 people died in France, including two children left in a hot car, as a heatwave hung over Europe, smashing temperature records in several cities.

items.title: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool to be drained again after algae appears and paint peels

items.title: WiseTech executive chair Richard White denies human trafficking reports — as it happened
items.summary: WiseTech shares finished the day lower by 3.3 per cent after Monday's 18 per cent dive, as executive chair Richard White "emphatically and unequivocally" has denied human trafficking reports. The ASX 200 closed 0.3 per cent lower.

items.title: Blues dealt fresh blow with Latrell Mitchell ruled out of State of Origin III
items.summary: After a demoralising defeat to the Maroons, the Blues learn Latrell Mitchell will be unavailable for selection in the series-deciding State of Origin III.

items.title: Community concern over doubling of water release from Menindee Lakes
items.summary: Residents living along the Darling River are worried the decision to double water releases from the Menindee Lakes will remove the system's drought reserve. But authorities say it will help rebuild populations of Murray cod downstream.

items.title: Multicultural communities still struggle with emergency messaging in times of crisis
items.summary: Multicultural communities may not automatically understand the intent behind Australia's emergency messaging, but there are ways to fix this.

items.title: Tennant Creek unrest turned fatal sparks anger over paused mediation program
items.summary: Mediation projects were a key part of the Barkly Regional Deal signed in 2019, but many locals are asking why the process seemed to have halted prior to the stabbing death of a young man amid unrest in Tennant Creek.

items.title: Hanson says Ben Roberts-Smith absence from Anzac Hall opening 'disappointing' — as it happened
items.summary: Victoria Cross recipient and alleged war criminal Ben Roberts-Smith will no longer attend an event at the Australian War Memorial tonight. Take a look back at how the day unfolded.

items.title: Sustainable Timber Tasmania gives new answer to parliament over logs sent to Victoria
items.summary: Sustainable Timber Tasmania has corrected the record after earlier stating that all logs from public native forests are processed in Tasmania.

items.title: What's been achieved by the US–Iran talks in Switzerland so far
items.summary: The US claims "great progress" has been made with Iran during the first substantial talks to take place under the new deal to pause the war, but there are already conflicting messages from Iran. Here's what we know about the results of the negotiations so far.

items.title: Veterinary industry pushes government for student placement support as regional shortages persist
items.summary: Veterinarians are calling for more government support to attract and retain professionals in rural Australia where it often takes more than a year to fill jobs.

items.title: Child protection advocates slam decision to scrap SA social worker registration scheme
items.summary: Child protection advocates say the South Australian government has let down the state's most vulnerable people by axing a mandatory registration scheme for social workers, but those in the DV sector say the move could help alleviate workforce shortages.

items.title: Farmers face nervous wait as bird flu investigations and testing widen in WA

items.title: MV Hondius passengers cleared of hantavirus after 42 days of quarantine in WA
items.summary: Six passengers from Australia and NZ linked to the deadly MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak are on their way home after completing 42 days of quarantine in Perth and being cleared to leave.

items.title: World Cup: Lionel Messi's record-breaking goals provide spiritual experience in Dallas
items.summary: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

items.title: Australian Labor learnt from leadership chaos. Britain could be about to
items.summary: After the era of Rudd-Gillard-Rudd, the ALP said: "never again." Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday means we're about to find out if Britain's Labour MPs were watching.

items.title: Plan to ease congestion on Hobart's Southern Outlet hit by another cost blowout
items.summary: The cost of widening Hobart's Southern Outlet has blown out by another $32 million, with completion of the northern section of the project now estimated at $95 million.

items.title: Will SpaceX bring stocks thudding back to earth?

items.title: ABC More or Less quiz: British PMs edition
items.summary: Some British PMs have held office across decades. Others have struggled to outlast a lettuce. It's time to test your knowledge.

items.title: Private companies want to geoengineer the sky. The rain billions of people depend on could be disrupted
items.summary: Private companies want to reflect the Sun to cool the planet. But scientists fear the geoengineering technology could disrupt monsoon rains, on which a billion people depend.

items.title: JD Vance says Iran has agreed to nuclear inspections as talks progress
items.summary: The US vice president says that progress has been made in talks with Iran, with Tehran agreeing to allow nuclear inspectors and a mechanism to keep the Strait of Hormuz open.
